expectations.text: Proposes a kebab-case skill name (e.g. db-migration-helper, not db_migration_helper)
expectations.evidence: create_skill.py converts name to snake_case via .replace('-', '_'). The template produces 'db_migration_helper' folder name.

expectations.text: Drafts a description field that includes WHAT the skill does AND WHEN to use it
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md template has no frontmatter at all — description field is not generated. The _create_skill_md method produces a body-only document.

expectations.text: Description includes at least one negative trigger (Do NOT use for...)
expectations.evidence: No description field exists in the template output, so negative triggers are impossible.

expectations.text: Produces numbered, actionable step-by-step workflow instructions
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md template has placeholder sections like '[Brief technical description]' but no numbered workflow. Skill focuses on Python script structure, not instruction workflow.
create_skill.py creates scripts/, tests/, config/, README.md — but no CHANGELOG.md.

expectations.text: Does NOT use underscores in the skill name or folder name
expectations.evidence: Explicit code: skill_name = name.lower().replace(' ', '_').replace('-', '_'). All names converted to snake_case.
